title: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtim
stakeholder_needs: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.
analysis: Stakeholders include end users (homeowners needing convenient quick-clean), purchasers (price/value, brand trust), regulators/market surveillance (EU safety/compliance), service/returns (repairability, battery replacement), manufacturing/supply chain (cost, component availability), and retailers (packaging, claims substantiation). Key requirement themes: performance (>=20 min runtime under defined mode/load), ergonomics (mass <1.5 kg including battery and dust cup; balance, grip, noise), safety/compliance (CE marking; LVD 2014/35/EU and EMC 2014/30/EU for charger/base, likely Radio Equipment Directive if wireless comms; RoHS, REACH; battery transport UN 38.3; IEC/EN 60335-1 and applicable part 2 for vacuum cleaners; battery pack safety IEC 62133-2; external power supply efficiency CoC/ErP). Environmental/operating conditions (indoor 10–40°C implies battery performance and charger thermal limits). Charging base requirements (input plug types for EU, cable length, stability, overcharge protection, charge time, indicators). Verification needs: define test method for runtime (mode, new filter, floor type, suction setting), weight measurement conditions, and safety tests (temperature rise, ingress, abnormal operation).
clarification_questions: How should the 20+ minute runtime be measured (power/suction mode, with/without motorized brush, clean vs partially clogged filter, on what surface/load)?
What cleaning performance is required beyond runtime (e.g., minimum suction power/airflow, pickup on hard floor vs carpet, dust capacity, filtration level such as HEPA)?
What charging expectations apply for the plug-in base (target charge time, battery type/capacity constraints, replaceable battery or sealed, and whether the vacuum must be usable while charging)?

table.title: DFMEA - Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner (EU, runtime >=20 min, mass <1.5 kg)
rows: Battery pack (Li-ion cells + BMS)
Provide energy for >=20 min runtime under defined mode/load
Capacity fade or insufficient delivered capacity
Runtime <20 min; customer dissatisfaction; returns; potential claim noncompliance
Performance
Cell quality variation; improper cell matching; high internal resistance; inadequate BMS cutoff calibration; aging at elevated temperature
Cell supplier qualification; cell matching spec; BMS calibration limits; derating vs temperature; incoming QC
Runtime test at EOL; pack IR test; BMS cutoff verification in production
Tighten cell IR/capacity limits; add pack-level capacity screening; optimize BMS cutoff and thermal derating; update runtime test definition and acceptance criteria
Battery Systems Engineer
After actions: O=4, D=4, S=7, RPN=112; runtime pass rate improved; field returns monitored
Battery pack (BMS protection)
Prevent overcharge/overdischarge/overcurrent per IEC 62133-2
BMS fails to interrupt overcurrent/short-circuit
Overheating; venting/fire risk; injury; regulatory noncompliance; recall
Safety Critical
MOSFET short; sense resistor open; firmware fault; inadequate fault coverage; ESD damage
Redundant hardware protection (fuse/PTC); robust MOSFET SOA design; firmware safety requirements; ESD design guidelines
Short-circuit and overcurrent protection verification; ICT for BMS; functional end-of-line protection test
Add secondary independent hardware cutoff (CID/thermal fuse); improve MOSFET derating and layout; expand fault-injection tests; strengthen ESD protection and validation
Safety/Compliance Engineer
After actions: O=2, D=3, S=10, RPN=60; protection test coverage increased
Charger / external power supply
Charge battery safely and within specified time; meet LVD/EMC/ErP
Overvoltage or incorrect CC/CV profile delivered
Battery overheating; reduced life; potential safety event; warranty cost
Safety Critical
Adapter regulation drift; wrong BOM/label mix; feedback loop instability; component tolerance stack-up
Approved PSU vendor list; keyed connectors; BOM control; design margining; supplier PPAP
Incoming inspection; charge voltage/current EOL check; periodic compliance re-test
Add PSU output monitoring during charge (pack-side); implement barcode-based PSU/BOM verification; tighten adapter output tolerances and audits
Power Electronics Engineer
After actions: O=2, D=3, S=9, RPN=54; misbuild risk reduced
Charging base / dock
Provide stable mechanical support and reliable electrical contact
Poor contact alignment or high contact resistance
Intermittent charging; extended charge time; heat at contacts; customer complaints
Reliability
Tolerance stack; spring contact fatigue; contamination/oxidation; base instability
Dock datum features; gold plating spec; contact force requirement; stability/anti-slip feet
Docking cycle test; contact resistance measurement; customer symptom diagnostics
Redesign contact geometry with self-aligning lead-in; increase contact wipe; add contamination shields; add production contact-resistance check
Mechanical Engineer
After actions: O=3, D=4, S=6, RPN=72; docking reliability improved
Motor (BLDC)
Generate airflow to meet suction performance within noise and thermal limits
Motor stalls or fails to start
No suction; potential overcurrent; reduced runtime; returns
Performance
Bearing seizure; winding defect; hall sensor failure; debris ingress; controller fault
Bearing spec and lubrication control; motor incoming QC; debris screens; controller current limit
EOL spin test; current signature analysis; acoustic/vibration check
Add motor current/position diagnostics; improve debris exclusion; tighten bearing supplier controls; increase stall protection robustness
Motor Design Engineer
After actions: O=3, D=4, S=8, RPN=96; stall-related returns reduced
Motor thermal management
Maintain temperatures within IEC/EN 60335 limits in normal/abnormal operation
Thermal runaway/overtemperature due to inadequate cooling
Surface too hot; burns; component damage; shutdown; compliance failure
Safety Critical
Blocked airflow; insufficient heatsinking; thermal sensor misplacement; fan curve not derated with filter clog
Thermal design review; derating strategy; thermal fuse; temperature sensor requirement and placement control
Temperature rise tests; abnormal operation tests (blocked nozzle/filter); production sensor calibration check
Add additional thermal sensor near hotspot; improve thermal path to housing; implement software derate with airflow estimation; verify worst-case blocked conditions
Thermal Engineer
After actions: O=2, D=3, S=10, RPN=60; margins increased vs limits
Suction path (nozzle/ducting)
Maintain airflow and suction with minimal pressure loss
Air leak in ducting joints
Reduced suction; higher noise; runtime reduction; poor cleaning performance
Performance
Poor assembly; warped parts; tolerance mismatch; seal missing/damaged
Assembly poka-yoke; joint design with interference; torque specs; gasket material spec
EOL suction/airflow test; leak check with pressure decay sampling
Improve joint features with snap + seal; add 100% pressure-decay leak test; enhance operator training and visual aids
Manufacturing Engineer
After actions: O=3, D=4, S=6, RPN=72; leak escapes reduced
Filter (primary + HEPA)
Capture dust and allergens while sustaining airflow
Filter clogs prematurely / high pressure drop
Reduced suction; runtime drop; overheating risk; customer dissatisfaction
Performance
Undersized filter area; media variability; incorrect user maintenance; dust cup design causes rapid loading
Filter sizing calculations; media supplier spec; user manual maintenance instructions; pre-filter stage
Airflow vs time test with standard dust; ΔP measurement; EOL airflow baseline check
Increase filter area or add cyclonic pre-separation; tighten media permeability spec; add clog indicator logic based on current/airflow; improve cleaning instructions
Airflow/CFD Engineer
After actions: O=4, D=4, S=7, RPN=112; clog complaints reduced
Filter installation interface
Ensure correct filter seating and bypass prevention
Filter not seated / bypass path opens
Dust passes to motor; motor wear; emissions; reduced suction; potential allergen exposure
Special Characteristic
User misassembly; weak latch; ambiguous orientation; seal compression set
Keyed geometry; positive latch force spec; clear markings; robust seal material
Misassembly test; EOL particulate bypass sampling; latch engagement verification
Redesign to foolproof keyed insertion; add tactile/audible latch; increase seal width and compression control; add user prompt in UI
Product Design Engineer
After actions: O=3, D=4, S=8, RPN=96; bypass events reduced
Housing/structure
Meet mass <1.5 kg while withstanding drops/handling
Housing cracks on drop
Sharp edges; internal damage; dust leakage; safety hazard; returns
Safety
Brittle material; thin walls; stress concentrators; poor weld/screw boss design; cold-temp embrittlement
Material selection and validation; FEA for drop; radius and rib guidelines; torque control
Drop tests; visual inspection; dimensional audits
Increase local wall thickness/ribs at hotspots; change resin or add impact modifier; improve boss design; implement drop-test gating at DV/PV
Mechanical/Structural Engineer
After actions: O=3, D=4, S=8, RPN=96; drop robustness improved
Seal (dust cup to body)
Prevent dust/air leakage and maintain suction
Seal compression set or wear over life
Gradual suction loss; dust leakage; higher noise; customer complaints
Reliability
Elastomer aging; chemical exposure; high compression; repeated removals; poor material spec
Material aging spec; compression set limits; design compression range; lubrication/finish control
Life-cycle removal test; periodic leak test; field return analysis
Select lower compression-set elastomer; optimize gland geometry; add wear indicator; validate with accelerated aging (heat/dust)
Materials Engineer
After actions: O=4, D=4, S=6, RPN=96; leakage over life reduced
Seal (battery compartment)
Prevent ingress and ensure electrical safety
Ingress of dust/moisture into battery/electronics
Corrosion; shorts; reduced life; potential safety event; intermittent faults
Safety Critical
Gasket damage; poor fit; vent path misdesign; user cleaning with damp cloth; tolerance stack
Gasket design and material spec; venting with labyrinth; assembly controls
Ingress tests; EOL functional test; visual inspection of gasket seating
Improve labyrinth/vent placement; add gasket retention features; add conformal coating on PCB; tighten gasket seating inspection criteria
Electronics Engineer
After actions: O=2, D=4, S=9, RPN=72; corrosion-related failures reduced
UI (buttons/trigger)
Provide reliable on/off and mode control with good ergonomics
Button/trigger sticks or fails
Cannot stop motor or cannot start; unsafe operation perception; returns
Safety
Debris ingress; weak spring; poor switch selection; housing interference
Switch qualification; debris shields; tolerance analysis; life-cycle requirement
Button life test; EOL functional test; customer feedback monitoring
Add boot/seal for trigger; increase clearances; select higher cycle-rated switch; add software long-press shutdown fallback
Human Factors Engineer
After actions: O=3, D=4, S=7, RPN=84; switch failures reduced
UI indicators (LEDs/display)
Communicate battery status, charging, and faults accurately
SOC indicator inaccurate or misleading
User perceives poor runtime; unexpected shutdown; increased returns; trust loss
Customer Satisfaction
Poor SOC algorithm; cell aging not compensated; LED calibration drift; voltage sag under load
SOC algorithm validation; calibration procedure; aging model; low-batt hysteresis
Runtime correlation testing; EOL indicator check; firmware unit tests
Implement coulomb counting with periodic OCV correction; add aging compensation; refine low-batt thresholds; expand validation across temps 10-40C
Firmware Engineer
After actions: O=4, D=4, S=5, RPN=80; indicator accuracy improved
EMC/ESD robustness (electronics)
Meet EMC Directive and maintain functionality under ESD
ESD causes reset or latch-up
Unexpected shutdown; potential overheating if control lost; compliance failure; returns
Safety
Insufficient TVS/grounding; poor cable shielding; layout coupling; enclosure discharge path inadequate
EMC design rules; TVS selection; proper grounding; shield strategy; pre-compliance testing
IEC 61000-4-2 ESD test; pre-scan EMC; production functional test
Improve PCB layout and grounding; add TVS on external interfaces; define controlled discharge points on housing; repeat EMC/ESD at worst-case builds
EMC Engineer
After actions: O=2, D=4, S=8, RPN=64; immunity improved
Weight / mass control (system)
Total mass <1.5 kg including battery and dust cup
Mass exceeds requirement due to tolerance creep/BOM changes
Ergonomics failure; retailer rejection; negative reviews; rework cost
Special Characteristic
Late ECO adds grams; supplier variation; fastener changes; lack of mass budget governance
Mass budget and tracking; ECO review includes mass delta; lightweight design guidelines
Weigh audit per lot; first article inspection; design reviews with mass roll-up
Implement strict mass budget with sign-off gates; add supplier mass tolerances; substitute lighter materials/fasteners; enforce weigh checks at PV
Program Manager
After actions: O=3, D=4, S=6, RPN=72; mass compliance stabilized
